title: 10 best herbal teas for red eyes
intro_desc: Herbal teas for red eyes are infusions made from plants that are known to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that help alleviate the symptoms of red eyes, such as dryness, itchiness, and swelling. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for red eyes include chamomile, calendula, and eyebright. Chamomile tea is well-known for its calming and anti-inflammatory properties, making it an ideal choice for soothing irritated eyes. Calendula tea, on the other hand, contains antioxidants that help reduce redness and inflammation. Eyebright tea is specifically formulated for eye health, and it has been used for centuries to treat various eye conditions, including red eyes. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can experience relief from red eyes, which can significantly improve their quality of life, especially for those who spend long hours staring at screens or work in dry environments.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garden orach tea helps with red eyes because it contains betalains, powerful antioxidants that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the eyes, thereby alleviating redness. Moreover, orach tea is rich in vitamin A, a nutrient essential for maintaining healthy eyes. Vitamin A helps protect the surface of the eye, reduces the risk of eye infections, and may help prevent dry eye symptoms, all of which can contribute to red eyes. Drinking orach tea can provide these nutrients and help soothe and relieve irritated, red eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Garden orach tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antioxidant: Its high antioxidant content, including vitamins A, C, and E, helps protect the eyes from damage and reduces redness caused by oxidative stress.
Astringent: The astringent properties of garden orach tea help to tighten and soothe irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and discomfort, due to the tannins present in the plant.
Analgesic: Garden orach tea contains analgesic compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with red eyes.
Immune-boosting: The immune-boosting properties of garden orach tea, attributed to its vitamin and mineral content, support overall eye health and help combat infections that can cause red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Plant organic orach seeds in well-drained soil, allowing 10 inches of space between each plant, and wait 4-6 weeks for the plants to reach maturity.
Harvest the orach leaves in the morning, choosing the younger, tender leaves for a milder flavor, and rinse them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Boil 2 cups of water and add 1 tablespoon of fresh orach leaves, reducing heat to a simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent leaves, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of orach tea per day, as needed, to relieve red eyes and soothe irritation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beetroot tea helps with red eyes because it is rich in antioxidants and vitamins that can improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. The tea's natural nitrates can help dilate blood vessels, reducing pressure in the eyes and alleviating redness. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of beetroot can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing the risk of red eyes. The tea's anti-inflammatory effects can also help soothe irritated eyes and reduce redness. Drinking beetroot tea regularly can help maintain healthy eyes and prevent redness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Beetroot tea contains betanin, which helps reduce inflammation, thus alleviating red eyes caused by irritation or infection.
Antioxidant: The high vitamin C content in beetroot tea fights oxidative stress, protecting the eyes from damage and reducing redness.
Vasodilatory: Beetroot tea's nitrate content promotes blood vessel dilation, increasing blood flow and reducing redness in the eyes.
Antimicrobial: The presence of betanin and other phenolic compounds in beetroot tea inhibits bacterial growth, helping to treat red eyes caused by bacterial infections.
Immune-boosting: Beetroot tea is rich in vitamins and minerals that support the immune system, helping the body fight off infections and reducing red eyes caused by inflammation.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of grated beetroot and 1 teaspoon of chamomile flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then add 1 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Serve the herbal beetroot tea while warm, up to 2 times a day for red eyes relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with red eyes because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation. The tea's antioxidants, including flavonoids and tannins, can help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can help to alleviate the appearance of red eyes. Additionally, white tea contains an abundance of vitamin C and E, which can help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing the risk of redness and irritation. White tea is a natural and gentle remedy for red eyes, providing a soothing and calming effect.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea contains flavonoids, such as catechins, that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce redness and irritation in the eyes caused by inflammation.
Antioxidant: White tea is rich in antioxidants, including epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, reducing redness and promoting overall eye health.
Anti-histamine: White tea has natural antihistamine properties due to the presence of flavonoids, which can help alleviate redness and itching in the eyes caused by allergies.
Vasoconstrictor: White tea contains tannins that act as vasoconstrictors, helping to narrow blood vessels and reduce redness in the eyes.
Immune support: White tea boosts the immune system, aiding in the prevention and treatment of eye infections that can cause redness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool to 175-185°F, the optimal temperature for white tea.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers and 1 teaspoon of dried calendula petals in the hot water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the herbs from the tea, leaving only the liquid.
Add 1 teaspoon of raw honey to the tea and stir until fully dissolved.
Let the tea cool down to a warm temperature before drinking to soothe red eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goosefoot tea helps with red eyes because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids. These compounds work to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels of the eyes, thereby reducing redness and irritation. Additionally, goosefoot tea is a natural diuretic, which helps to eliminate toxins from the body and improve overall eye health. The tea's soothing properties also help to alleviate dryness and discomfort, providing relief for red, tired eyes. Drinking goosefoot tea regularly can help to maintain healthy eyes and prevent redness from occurring in the first place.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Goosefoot tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation, which is important for red eyes as inflammation can cause blood vessels in the eyes to dilate, leading to redness.
Antihistamine: Goosefoot tea contains antihistamine properties that can help alleviate allergic reactions, which can cause red, itchy eyes.
Antioxidant: Goosefoot tea is rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin C and E, that can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to red eyes.
Astringent: Goosefoot tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness.
Analgesic: Goosefoot tea contains analgesic compounds that can help relieve pain and discomfort associated with red eyes, providing soothing relief.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried goosefoot herb and place it in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 2-3 minutes until it reaches a temperature of 205-212°F.
Pour the hot water over the goosefoot herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed for red eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tea helps with red eyes because it contains bioflavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. These compounds can help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes by shrinking blood vessels and soothing inflammation. Additionally, eyebright tea has antimicrobial properties that can help protect the eyes from infection, which can also cause redness. Drinking eyebright tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from red eyes and promote overall eye health. However, it is important to note that eyebright tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for serious eye conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tea reduces inflammation, which is important for red eyes as inflammation can cause blood vessels in the eye to expand and become more visible. Active constituents include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Astringent: Eyebright tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness. This is due to the presence of tannins, which have a drying effect on mucous membranes.

Antimicrobial: Eyebright tea has antimicrobial properties that can help combat eye infections, which can cause redness. This is attributed to the presence of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils.

Antioxidant: Eyebright tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to redness. The main antioxidant compounds found in eyebright include flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Analgesic: Eyebright tea has analgesic properties, which can help alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with red eyes. This is due to the presence of alkaloids, which have a numbing effect.
remedy_recipe: In a heatproof bowl, combine 1 teaspoon of dried eyebright herb with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture, covered, for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to be released.
Strain the tea to remove the herb particles, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Squeeze a slice of lemon (optional) into the tea for added vitamin C and a refreshing taste.
Drink the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe and reduce redness in your eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with red eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate eye irritation and reduce redness. Fennel contains a compound called flavonoids, which are known to have antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by environmental stressors. Additionally, fennel tea can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels surrounding the eyes, which can help reduce redness and puffiness. Drinking fennel tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from red eyes and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes by reducing swelling and soothing irritated tissue. This is due to the presence of compounds like anethole, limonene, and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in fennel tea helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to red eyes. Active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid, quercetin, and various vitamins.

Antimicrobial: Fennel tea's antimicrobial properties help prevent and treat eye infections that can cause redness. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole.

Astringent: Fennel tea's astringent properties help tighten and soothe the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness. This is due to the presence of tannins, which help constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid leakage.

Analgesic: Fennel tea's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with red eyes. This is due to the presence of compounds like limonene, anethole, and various flavonoids, which have pain-relieving effects.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Crush 1 teaspoon of fennel seeds and add them to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the hot water over the crushed fennel seeds and steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the fennel seeds.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking, allowing the herbal properties to soothe red eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with red eyes because it contains an active compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Red eyes are often a sign of inflammation in the blood vessels of the eye. Drinking licorice tea can help reduce this inflammation, thereby alleviating redness and discomfort. Additionally, licorice tea is known to have soothing and cooling effects, which can further help to reduce redness and irritation in the eyes. However, it is important to note that while licorice tea can be helpful in relieving red eyes, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in cases of severe or persistent redness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea contains glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, relieving redness caused by irritation or inflammation.
Immune system support: Licorice tea has antiviral and antibacterial properties, which help strengthen the immune system and prevent eye infections that can lead to red eyes.
Antioxidant: Licorice tea is rich in flavonoids and chalcones, which have antioxidant properties that protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, reducing redness.
Vasoconstrictor: Licorice tea contains isoflavones, which act as vasoconstrictors, helping to narrow blood vessels in the eyes and reducing redness caused by dilated blood vessels.
Antihistamine: Licorice tea has antihistamine properties due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, which can help alleviate red eyes caused by allergies by inhibiting the release of histamines.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried licorice root.
Lower the heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the licorice root solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, for relief of red eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with red eyes because it contains properties that are anti-inflammatory and astringent, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The tannins in witch hazel can also help to soothe redness and irritation by constricting blood vessels in the eyes. Additionally, witch hazel has antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Drinking witch hazel tea can provide relief for red eyes, while using it as a compress can also help reduce redness and irritation. Overall, witch hazel tea is a natural and effective remedy for red eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea helps reduce red eyes by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its tannin and volatile oil content.
Astringent: Its astringent properties, due to the presence of tannins, help soothe irritated eyes and reduce redness.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, primarily from flavonoids, helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage.
Antimicrobial: Witch hazel tea's antimicrobial properties, from its gallic acid and tannins, help prevent eye infections that can cause redness.
Vasoconstrictor: Witch hazel tea contains active compounds, such as gallic acid and proanthocyanidins, that can help constrict blood vessels, reducing redness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried witch hazel leaves, letting it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the tea steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea daily, as needed, to soothe red eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common sunflower tea helps with red eyes because it contains properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation. The tea is rich in antioxidants, particularly flavonoids, which can help alleviate redness and soothe irritated eyes. Additionally, sunflower tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from symptoms of dryness and fatigue. The tea can be consumed or used as a compress to help reduce redness and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sunflower tea helps reduce red eyes by alleviating inflammation, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in sunflower tea, including vitamin E, helps protect and soothe irritated eyes.
Antimicrobial: Sunflower tea's antimicrobial properties, attributed to its rich polyphenol content, help combat potential eye infections causing redness.
Astringent: The astringent nature of sunflower tea, due to tannins, helps tighten and soothe swollen, red eyes.
Soothing: Sunflower tea provides a soothing effect on red eyes, as it contains various calming compounds, such as essential fatty acids and amino acids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried common sunflower petals.
Place the sunflower petals in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the petals and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the infuser or strain the petals from the tea.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for red eye relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle tea helps with red eyes because it contains high levels of antioxidants, vitamin C, and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, thereby alleviating redness. Additionally, roselle tea has been shown to improve circulation, which can help increase the supply of oxygen and nutrients to the eyes, further reducing redness and promoting overall eye health. The tea's soothing and cooling effects can also help relieve eye strain and fatigue, providing additional relief for red eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Roselle tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes by decreasing swelling and irritation in the blood vessels of the eyes. This is due to the presence of anthocyanins and other polyphenols.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in roselle tea helps protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of developing red eyes. This is attributed to the presence of vitamin C, flavonoids, and other polyphenols.

Antihistamine: Roselle tea has natural antihistamine properties that help alleviate allergic reactions, including those that cause red eyes. This is due to the presence of quercetin and other flavonoids.

Astringent: Roselle tea has astringent properties that help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes by tightening the skin and reducing the appearance of bloodshot eyes. This is due to the presence of tannins.

Analgesic: Roselle tea has natural pain-relieving properties that help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with red eyes. This is due to the presence of several compounds, including epicatechin and other flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Carefully measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried roselle flowers.
Steep - Place the roselle flowers in a tea infuser and steep in boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain - Remove the infuser and strain the tea to remove any remaining flower particles.
Sweeten (optional) - Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired.
Serve - Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking to soothe red eyes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat red eyes with herbal teas is by using chamomile or green tea bags. Steep the tea bags in hot water for several minutes, then let them cool down until they're comfortably warm. Gently place the tea bags over your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. Both chamomile and green tea contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation. Remember to always test the temperature of the tea bags before applying them to your eyes to avoid burns. If redness persists, consult a healthcare professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to red eyes that are treated with herbal teas include dry eye, eye strain, and conjunctivitis. Chamomile tea is a popular choice for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help alleviate redness and irritation. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, can reduce eye puffiness and dark circles. For dry eye, fennel or eyebright tea can provide relief by increasing tear production and reducing inflammation. When using herbal teas for eye ailments, it is important to ensure the tea is properly cooled and to avoid using tea bags that have been previously steeped, as they can harbor bacteria. Always consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
